critic_raw:
## ML Critique (Run 2): Joint Transformer vs. TF-IDF for ICD-10 Code Suggestion

**Issue 1 — Micro-F1 primary metric fails the lower-frequency-code clinical objective**

The design aims to 'improve performance across the full spectrum of frequent codes, especially those in the lower-frequency half of the distribution.' Micro-F1 aggregates predictions across all codes with equal weight per prediction — meaning frequent codes, having far more predictions, dominate. A code appearing in 5% of encounters contributes about 10x fewer predictions than a code appearing in 50% of encounters. Micro-F1 improvement from the lower-frequency half is drowned out by performance on the high-frequency codes. Macro-F1 (equal weight per code regardless of frequency) is the correct metric for an objective that explicitly prioritizes coverage of lower-frequency codes.
defender_raw:
## Defense (Run 2)

**Issue 1:** Concede. Macro-F1 should be primary for a lower-frequency code coverage objective. Micro-F1 is dominated by frequent codes and will not detect lower-frequency improvements.
